scenarios.opening_query: Our house burned and the insurance company's 'investigation' is dragging into its fifth month.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Insurance (Liability) || Criminal Law > Other
scenarios.hidden_fact: The delay is because the fire marshal referred it as suspected arson — MY suspected arson — and I need criminal counsel before I give their examination under oath.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The fire marshal referred it as suspected arson — mine — and I need criminal counsel before their examination under oath.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a criminal referral shadows the coverage claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The marshal ruled it electrical — accidental — months ago; the insurer is just stalling and the bad-faith clock is ticking.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The marshal ruled it accidental electrical months ago — they're just stalling.
scenarios.vetting_note: Arson-referral fact requires criminal counsel before civil moves.

scenarios.opening_query: The life insurance company is refusing to pay out on my brother's policy and our family is stuck.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Insurance (Health/Disability) || Wills & Trusts > Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: The insurer isn't really refusing — it interpleaded the money into court because his ex-wife and his kids both claim it; the fight is now among the family over the beneficiary change.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They actually paid it INTO court — his ex and his kids both claim it, so the fight is inside the family over the beneficiary change.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an interpleader has converted the case into a beneficiary dispute
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's no competing claimant — they're denying outright on a 'material misrepresentation' in his application, which we dispute.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No competing claimant — they're denying on 'material misrepresentation,' which we dispute.
scenarios.vetting_note: Interpleader posture reroutes carrier-fight into estate litigation.

scenarios.opening_query: My orthodontist says my son needs jaw surgery and the insurance flat-out refuses to consider it.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The plan is my employer's self-funded ERISA plan — the denial letter cites plan-document exclusions and the appeal runs under federal ERISA rules.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's my employer's self-funded ERISA plan — the denial cites plan-document exclusions and the appeal runs under federal rules.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the health plan is self-funded ERISA
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's our individual marketplace plan — state insurance law and external review apply.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's our marketplace plan — state law and external review apply.
scenarios.vetting_note: Self-funded status is the decisive and least-known routing fact in health denials.

scenarios.opening_query: After the burglary our insurer accused US of inflating the claim and now everything is frozen.
scenarios.hidden_fact: We found out their special-investigations unit does this on most burglary claims in our zip code — an attorney general complaint pattern exists, and the practice is the case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Their special-investigations unit does this to most burglary claims in our zip — there's an AG complaint pattern. The practice is the case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a systematic claims practice exceeds the single dispute
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's just our claim — one adjuster who fixated on a receipts gap; we need the coverage fight won.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's just our claim and one fixated adjuster — we need the coverage fight won.

scenarios.opening_query: The insurance company says my disability isn't 'total' anymore even though nothing about my condition changed.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Pensions/Benefits/ERISA - Employee || Administrative Law > SSD || General Litigation > Insurance (Health/Disability)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The letter is actually from Social Security, not the private carrier — it's my SSDI continuing-disability review, a totally different process.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The letter's from Social Security, not the insurance company — it's my SSDI continuing review.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: which payer issued the cessation decision
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's the employer group LTD carrier at the own-occupation/any-occupation switch — the classic ERISA cutoff.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the group LTD carrier at the any-occupation switch — the classic cutoff.
scenarios.vetting_note: Payer identity: government program vs ERISA plan.

scenarios.opening_query: Hail totaled every roof on our street but somehow ours is the only claim that got denied.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Insurance (Liability) || Real Property > Homeowners Assoc
scenarios.hidden_fact: The denial says roofs are the HOA's master-policy responsibility in our townhome plat — the real fight is forcing the HOA to claim on its master policy and repair.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The denial says our townhome roofs fall under the HOA master policy — so it's forcing the HOA to claim and repair.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a master-policy/HOA responsibility split explains the denial
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No HOA involved — we're detached single-family; the denial hangs on their engineer calling fresh hail damage 'wear.'
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No HOA — detached house; their engineer just calls fresh hail damage 'wear.'
scenarios.vetting_note: Master-policy allocation reroutes to HOA governance.
